threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still refer to the Mare Street traffic lights as 'that roundabout' instead of just knowing the chaos around them.
Caught the N37 last week—saw a guy moonwalk along the aisle. Whole bus erupted in laughter like it was a show. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the guy juggling flaming torches at Hermannplatz while a group of kids cheered him on. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find spontaneous parties in the U-Bahn, complete with DJs and dancing, as if the train is just a giant club on wheels.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a true local when they still call it 'The Mission' and not just 'Mission District' like the techies from the East Bay.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Nothing captures Muni like that moment when the bus is full, but the driver still stops to let on the guy with the accordion. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals drink wine at every meal, but trust me, you'll see more cerveses in Gràcia's terraces than any fancy bottle.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their struggle to find the right 'pa amb tomàquet' at the corner bars; locals know it’s not just a menu item.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'Haggerston Station' instead of 'Haggerston' like the locals. It’s just Haggerston, mate.
Last night, heard two lads arguing about the best fried chicken in Brixton - one swore by Morley's, the other yelled 'cluck off!' at the mention of KFC.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: It hit me when I saw a group of kids selling home-made lemonade outside Lidl, chatting about their favorite flavors like they were royalty.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can have a midnight snack at an open-air Turkish market, surrounded by graffiti murals—where else do street vendors serve döner to the sound of live DJs?

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their knack for dodging the dog droppings on 24th Street, while the newcomers are too busy staring at their phones.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 29 right as a fog rolled in, full of people sharing their weekend stories. Muni's a strange mix of the mundane and magical here.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is all about the parks and plazas, but they miss the local bakeries where the real community gathers for morning coffee.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still stare at the menu in cafes, waiting for someone to explain tapas. No one does—just order the pan con tomate.
